headline: Claude Fable 5 anchored the quarter, with operational efficiency the lone improving mover as guardrails drew sustained backlash.
body: Operational efficiency was the quarter's strongest gainer (momentum 145) while most technique concepts lagged, including AI-assisted coding, prompt caching, and cache hit rate, and AI layer design and encrypted reasoning weakened. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 dominated the new items: its tier routing system, agentic payment and memory features, and Claude Code workflows recurred [3][5][8][11], even as users reported refused prompts, false safety alarms, and suspicion of downgraded model fallbacks [7]. Adjacent tooling pushed agent integration deeper into apps, from CopilotKit rendering real UI with human-in-the-loop approval [0] to MCP-based agent configuration on enterprise data [10]. Cost discipline surfaced as a theme, from a $20,000 API key incident [2] to roughly $2-per-video automated production pipelines [11].
